description_long: This object is a vintage wooden radio receiver with a rectangular form and a classic mid-century design. The front features a large, white mesh speaker grille occupying the upper half, flanked by two round white knobs on either side, and a black frequency display window in the center. Below the display are several rectangular white push buttons, likely for station selection or power control. The sides and back are made of smooth, polished wood with a warm brown finish, and the back is plain with no visible openings or controls. The overall construction suggests a solid, boxy build typical of radios from the mid-20th century, with a focus on both functionality and decorative appeal. This radio would be a striking feature in an urban setting, such as a retro-themed café, a vintage electronics shop, or as a collectible in a residential garage.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a large white mesh speaker grille, two round knobs, a central frequency display, and several white push buttons, all set in a polished brown wooden case.
description_view_0: The front view shows the white mesh speaker grille, two round white knobs, a central black frequency display, and a row of white push buttons.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the smooth, uninterrupted wooden panel with no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a plain wooden surface with no visible openings, controls, or branding.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing a smooth wooden panel without any controls or features.
